# FoAIMDL-Lab-3: Setting Up a Project from Scratch

*This .README file has been generated by Github Copilot.*

A complete, production-ready machine learning project template demonstrating how to structure a deep learning experiment from the ground up. This lab implements image classification on the Tiny ImageNet-200 dataset using PyTorch and Weights & Biases (WandB) for experiment tracking.

## 📋 Project Overview

This project serves as a practical guide to setting up a machine learning workflow, including:
- **Model Architecture**: Custom neural network implementation for 200-class image classification
- **Training Pipeline**: Full training loop with validation and checkpointing
- **Evaluation**: Comprehensive model evaluation on test data
- **Experiment Tracking**: Integration with Weights & Biases for logging metrics and monitoring
- **Data Management**: Structured data loading with train/validation/test splits

## 🗂️ Project Structure

```
FoAIMDL-Lab-3/
├── train.py              # Training script with WandB integration
├── eval.py               # Evaluation script for testing trained models
├── requirements.txt      # Python dependencies
├── checkpoints/          # Directory for saving model weights
├── data/                 # Data loading utilities
├── dataset/              # Tiny ImageNet-200 dataset location
├── models/               # Custom model definitions
│   └── CustomNet         # 200-class image classification model
└── utils/                # Utility functions
```

## 🔧 Requirements

- Python 3.7+
- CUDA-compatible GPU (recommended, CPU supported)

## 📦 Installation

1. Clone the repository:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/AgricolaC/FoAIMDL-Lab-3.git
cd FoAIMDL-Lab-3
```

2. Install dependencies:
```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

3. Set up Weights & Biases (optional but recommended):
```bash
wandb login
```

## 📚 Dependencies

- **torch** - PyTorch framework for deep learning
- **torchvision** - Pre-trained models and utilities for computer vision
- **wandb** - Experiment tracking and visualization
- **matplotlib** - Plotting and visualization
- **pandas** - Data manipulation and analysis
- **numpy** - Numerical computing
- **requests** - HTTP library for data downloading
- **tqdm** - Progress bars for iterative processes

## 🚀 Usage

### Training the Model

Run the training script to start training on Tiny ImageNet-200:

```bash
python train.py
```

**Training Configuration:**
- Learning Rate: 0.001
- Batch Size: 64
- Epochs: 10
- Optimizer: SGD with momentum (0.9)
- Loss Function: CrossEntropyLoss
- Device: Automatically selects GPU if available, otherwise CPU

**Key Features:**
- Automatic checkpoint saving of the best-performing model
- Real-time metric logging to WandB (loss, accuracy, validation accuracy)
- Progress tracking with TQDM
- GPU/CPU device detection

### Evaluating the Model

After training, evaluate the best model on the test set:

```bash
python eval.py
```

**What it does:**
1. Loads the best saved model from `checkpoints/best_model.pth`
2. Evaluates on the test set from Tiny ImageNet-200
3. Reports final loss and accuracy metrics

## 📊 Experiment Tracking with WandB

This project uses Weights & Biases for comprehensive experiment tracking. During training, the following metrics are logged:
- `train_loss` - Training loss per epoch
- `train_acc` - Training accuracy per epoch
- `val_acc` - Validation accuracy per epoch
- `final_test_acc` - Final test set accuracy

View your experiments at: https://wandb.ai/

## 📁 Key Directories

| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `checkpoints/` | Stores trained model weights (best_model.pth) |
| `data/` | Contains data loading utilities (tiny_imagenet module) |
| `dataset/` | Location for Tiny ImageNet-200 dataset |
| `models/` | Custom model architecture (CustomNet for 200 classes) |
| `utils/` | Utility functions and helpers |

## 🎯 Model Architecture

**CustomNet**: A custom neural network designed for 200-class image classification on Tiny ImageNet-200, supporting:
- GPU acceleration (CUDA)
- Batch normalization for stable training
- Flexible architecture with configurable class count

## 💡 Key Concepts Demonstrated

1. **Project Organization**: Proper separation of concerns with modular code
2. **Device Handling**: Automatic GPU detection and fallback to CPU
3. **Best Practice Checkpointing**: Saving model weights when validation improves
4. **Experiment Tracking**: Integration with WandB for reproducibility
5. **Validation Loop**: Proper evaluation without gradient computation
6. **Progress Monitoring**: User-friendly progress bars and logging

## 🔄 Typical Workflow

1. **Setup**: Install dependencies and prepare environment
2. **Download Data**: Obtain Tiny ImageNet-200 dataset in `dataset/` folder
3. **Train**: Run `train.py` to begin training with WandB logging
4. **Monitor**: Track metrics in real-time via WandB dashboard
5. **Evaluate**: Run `eval.py` to assess final model performance

## 📝 Notes

- Ensure the `dataset/tiny-imagenet-200/` directory is properly set up before training
- The training script automatically creates the `checkpoints/` directory
- GPU availability is auto-detected; training runs on CUDA if available
- Model checkpoints are saved when validation accuracy improves
- All metrics are logged to WandB for easy visualization and comparison. USE YOUR OWN API KEY!

## 🔗 Resources

- [PyTorch Documentation](https://pytorch.org/docs/)
- [Tiny ImageNet Dataset](http://cs231n.stanford.edu/tiny-imagenet-200.zip)
- [Weights & Biases](https://wandb.ai/)
- [Torch Vision](https://pytorch.org/vision/)

## ✨ About This Lab

This is Lab 3 of the "Foundations of AI and Machine Learning" course, demonstrating best practices for setting up a production-ready ML project from scratch. It serves as a template for future machine learning experiments.
